description: USAN DPS Alert email templates - state-aware with actual DPP links and compliance references

templates.name: No Response - California
templates.body_template:
{{rep_name}}, please review the form below and reach out to the customer listed to resolve the issue.

Please monitor the tickets that require Electronic Positive Responses within the Damage Prevention Portal (DPP) as required by California Code 4216 (https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displayText.xhtml?lawCode=GOV&division=5.&title=1.&part=&chapter=3.1.&article=2).

• How to use Electronic Positive Response - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=c1b6HlX-fx4

Thank you,

templates.body_template:
{{rep_name}}, please review the form below and reach out to the customer listed to resolve the issue.

Please monitor the tickets that require Electronic Positive Responses within the Damage Prevention Portal (DPP).

templates.name: Contact Info Issue
templates.body_template:
{{rep_name}}, please review the email below from one of our agents for reference and reach out to the excavator on the ticket to resolve the issue.

If you need to update your Member Contact information within the Damage Prevention Portal, please use the link below to sign in. Then navigate to the station details for your station code (Settings > OneCallAccess).

NV DPP Login Page - https://appsnv.undergroundservicealert.org/pcadmin/Account/Login
CA DPP Login Page - https://appsca.undergroundservicealert.org/pcadmin/Account/Login?returnUrl=%2Fpcadmin%2FOperations - The Damage Prevention Portal is where you can manage your mapping, contact info, and ticket delivery.

Contact Information can be found under Settings - OneCallAccess then clicking on your Station Code.
The Member Contact is displayed on each ticket available for the excavator.
The Member IT contact is not displayed on the ticket and is meant for the Member Rep.

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Thank you,

templates.name: Member Manually Added
templates.body_template:
Hello {{rep_name}},

Please review the email below from one of our agents for reference. The station code {{station_codes}} has been manually added to ticket# {{ticket_number}} at the request of the excavator.

If you need to update your Area of Interest, please visit the Damage Prevention Portal using the link below.

CA DPP Login Page - https://appsca.undergroundservicealert.org/pcadmin/Account/Login?returnUrl=%2Fpcadmin%2FOperations - The Damage Prevention Portal is where you can manage your mapping, contact info, and ticket delivery.

Area of Interest (AOI) can be found under Settings - OneCallAccess and clicking on your Station Code, then clicking on Area of Interest.
Here is a video that goes into more detail: How to manage your Area of Interest - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KZzHnITi-3Q&feature=youtu.be

Thank you,
